definition: The reason for which something is done or created or for which something exists.
example: Purpose is that feeling that you are part of something bigger than yourself... Purpose is what creates true happiness.

definition: The state of being equal, especially in status, rights, and opportunities.
example: Every generation expands its definition of equality.

definition: The practice of forming or pursuing ideals, especially unrealistically.
example: Idealism is good fuel—but be prepared to be misunderstood; anyone with a big vision will be.

definition: A group of people living in the same place or having a particular characteristic in common.
example: Community change starts local; even global shifts begin small—with people like us.

definition: A written or spoken agreement, especially one concerning employment, sales, or tenancy, that is intended to be enforceable by law.
example: Previous generations fought for the vote and civil rights. They had the New Deal and Great Society. And now it's time for our generation to define a new social contract.

definition: Having meaning or significance; important or worthwhile.
example: By taking on big meaningful projects together, by redefining equality so everyone has the freedom to pursue purpose, and by building community across the world.
It's really good to be idealistic. But be prepared to be misunderstood.

definition: Characterized by idealism; unrealistically aiming for perfection.
example: It's really good to be idealistic. But be prepared to be misunderstood. Anyone working on a big vision is going to get called crazy.

definition: A relationship in which people or things are linked.
example: The bet was simple and vast: connection at global scale can still start with one campus network.

definition: Forward movement toward a destination or improved condition.
example: Progress is uneven—defend the direction even when the week looks ugly.

definition: The state of being accountable for something within one’s power.
example: Scale without responsibility is just a larger blast radius.

definition: An important assignment or purpose undertaken by a group.
example: A mission statement is worthless until it forces trade-offs in the product roadmap.

definition: A firmly held belief or opinion.
example: Conviction without evidence is noise; conviction after struggle is leadership.

definition: The quality of being coherent and intelligible.
example: Clarity is kindness in speech—confusion is often a way to hide.

definition: The quality of being honest and having strong moral principles.
example: Integrity is what you do when the speech ends and no one is applauding.

definition: The act of giving up something valued for the sake of other considerations.
example: Sacrifice is the receipt attached to every lasting freedom.
